description_long: This object is a bunch of five bananas joined at the stem, with each banana displaying a smooth, slightly curved form typical of the species Musa. The peels are predominantly yellow with some brown and black blemishes, especially near the tips and along the sides, indicating natural bruising and handling marks. The stem is thick, dark, and fibrous, showing where the bananas were once attached to a larger bunch. The bananas are closely packed, with their sides pressed together, and the tips are slightly pointed and darkened. The surface of the bananas is matte with a subtle sheen, and the texture appears semi-soft with minor ridges and indentations. No commercial stickers or labels are visible on any of the bananas.
description: A bunch of five yellow bananas joined at the stem, with some brown blemishes and a matte, slightly textured peel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cluster of five bananas, with the stem and tips clearly visible and the bananas fanned out.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the bananas and more pronounced brown blemishes along the sides.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the rounded backs of the bananas and the darkened stem area.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the underside of the bunch and the close packing of the bananas.
attribute_peel_condition: The banana peels show some brown and black blemishes, especially near the tips and along the sides, with minor bruising but no major tears or splits. The surface appears intact with a slightly rough texture.
